skill.name: agentic-workflow
skill.description: Practical AI agent workflows and productivity techniques. Provides optimized patterns for daily development tasks such as commands, shortcuts, Git integration, MCP usage, and session management.
skill.summary: A comprehensive guide for optimizing AI agent productivity with commands, shortcuts, and multi-agent workflows.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. This is a documentation/guide skill providing productivity tips for AI agents (Claude Code, Gemini, Codex). The scanner detected markdown table delimiters as shell commands, standard Docker install URLs as malicious URLs, git worktree syntax as path traversal, and environment variable references as credential access. No actual security risks present.

critical_findings.title: Prompt Injection Attempt Detected
critical_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE - No prompt injection present. Static scanner triggered on markdown documentation format.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Table Delimiters Detected as Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: The static scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns at lines 31-77, 84-261. These are markdown table delimiters (|) and slash commands (/init, /usage, /clear) in documentation, not executable shell commands. This is a documentation skill, not executable code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown tables clearly documented as slash commands for Claude Code. No backticks or shell execution present.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Official Claude Installation URL
medium_findings.description: The scanner detected 'Hardcoded URL' at line 214: https://claude.ai/install.sh. This is the official Claude CLI installation script - legitimate and safe.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Official Anthropic installation URL for Claude CLI. Standard installation method documented by vendor.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Git Worktree Syntax
medium_findings.description: The scanner detected 'Path traversal sequence' at lines 123-124. This is standard git worktree syntax (../myapp-feature-auth), not a path traversal vulnerability.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard git worktree command using relative path for branch directory. Common workflow for multi-branch development.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Shell Configuration File Reference
medium_findings.description: The scanner detected 'Hidden file in home directory' at line 97. This is ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc - a documentation comment about shell configuration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard documentation for shell alias configuration. Common practice for developer productivity.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Docker Environment Variable Reference
medium_findings.description: The scanner detected 'Generic API/secret keys' at line 225. The $ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is a Docker environment variable reference, not a hardcoded secret.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard Docker pattern for passing existing environment variables into containers. Best practice for secret management.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Pipe to Shell Pattern
medium_findings.description: The scanner detected 'Pipe to shell pattern' at line 214 (curl | sh). This is the standard official method for installing Claude CLI.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Official Claude installation method from Anthropic documentation. Industry-standard installation approach.

content.value_statement: Learn optimized workflows for AI agents like Claude Code, Gemini, and Codex. This skill provides commands, shortcuts, and multi-agent patterns to boost daily development productivity.
actual_capabilities: Navigate keyboard shortcuts (Esc Esc to cancel, Ctrl+R for history, Shift+Tab for plan mode)
Manage Claude Code sessions with --continue and --resume flags
Integrate Git workflows using AI for commit messages and PR creation
Configure MCP servers for multi-agent orchestration (Playwright, Supabase, Firecrawl)
Set up Docker containers with Claude CLI for safe experimentation
limitations: Skill provides documentation only, not executable automation
Some commands are Claude Code specific and may not work in other AI assistants
Requires understanding of command-line tools and Git workflows

use_cases.title: Developer productivity boost
use_cases.description: Learn keyboard shortcuts and slash commands to navigate Claude Code faster, reducing context switching time.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers using Claude Code daily

use_cases.title: Multi-agent orchestration
use_cases.description: Set up workflows combining Claude for planning, Gemini for large-scale analysis, and Codex for command execution.
use_cases.target_user: Advanced users managing complex AI-assisted projects

use_cases.title: Git workflow automation
use_cases.description: Use AI agents to auto-generate commit messages, create PRs, and manage git worktrees for parallel development.
use_cases.target_user: Developers working with Git and GitHub daily

prompt_templates.title: Check session status
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the current token usage and context window status using the appropriate Claude Code command.

prompt_templates.title: Auto-commit changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the current git changes and write an appropriate commit message, then commit them.

prompt_templates.title: Create Docker sandbox
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a Docker container with Claude Code installed for safe experimentation. Include instructions for mounting the workspace.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-agent code review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the multi-agent pattern: ask Gemini to analyze the codebase structure, then have Codex run tests on the changes.

output_examples.input: How do I cancel a running task in Claude Code?
output_examples.output: Press Esc Esc to cancel the last task immediately. This is the highest priority shortcut in Claude Code.

output_examples.input: What MCP servers should I use for browser automation?
output_examples.output: Use the Playwright MCP server for controlling web browsers. It is designed for E2E testing and browser automation tasks.

output_examples.input: How can I work on multiple branches simultaneously?
output_examples.output: Use git worktrees: 'git worktree add ../myapp-feature-auth feature/auth' creates an independent working directory for each branch. Run separate AI sessions per worktree.

best_practices: Use /clear or /context regularly to prevent context pollution and maintain performance
Keep MCP servers under 10 and active tools under 80 for optimal performance
Combine agents strategically: Claude for planning/coding, Gemini for large-scale analysis, Codex for running commands
anti_patterns: Avoid running Claude in a single session for weeks without reset - context degrades over time
Do not enable too many MCP servers simultaneously - causes tool selection confusion
Avoid using AI for simple tasks that are faster done manually - context switching overhead

faq.question: What is the most important shortcut in Claude Code?
faq.answer: Esc Esc is the highest priority shortcut. It cancels the last task immediately when pressed twice.

faq.question: How do I continue a previous conversation?
faq.answer: Use 'claude --continue' to continue the last conversation, or 'claude --resume <session-name>' to resume a specific session.

faq.question: Which MCP servers should I start with?
faq.answer: Start with Playwright for browser automation, Supabase for database access, and Firecrawl for web scraping. Keep total under 10 servers.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Gemini or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Codex CLI commands. Many patterns work across all three tools.

faq.question: How do I set up a safe experimentation environment?
faq.answer: Use the Docker container setup in the skill. Build with 'docker build -t ai-sandbox .' and run with environment variables passed via -e flags.

faq.question: What is the recommended multi-agent workflow?
faq.answer: Use orchestration pattern: Claude for planning and code generation, Gemini for large-scale codebase analysis, Codex for running build and test commands.
